title: CIP-309 chunked URI text array
description: A CBOR `[1* tstr .size (1..64)]`: a non-empty array of text-string chunks, each 1..64 bytes, that concatenate to a single absolute URI (e.g. ar://, ipfs://). The `.size` bound is a BYTE-count constraint on the UTF-8 encoding of each chunk, not a code-unit count. A zero-length chunk is rejected with the same CHUNK_TOO_LARGE code as an oversized chunk. STRUCTURAL only: the reconstructed URI's absoluteness, fragment-freeness, allowed scheme set (ar://, ipfs://), and per-scheme body shape (including the IPFS CID profile) are domain-pass checks (INVALID_URI), not expressible here. Note: the `maxLength: 64` below counts UTF-16/Unicode characters, so multi-byte UTF-8 chunks at the 64-byte boundary are admitted permissively here and bounded exactly by the validator.
